serviceDescription: Amazon API Gateway is a fully managed service that makes it easy for developers to create, publish, maintain, monitor, and secure APIs at any scale. APIs act as the front door for applications to access data, business logic, or functionality from your backend services.

cards.title: HTTP APIs
cards.helpText: With HTTP APIs, you only pay when your APIs are in use. There are no minimum fees or upfront commitments. You pay for the API calls you receive and the amount of data transferred out. Requests are metered in 512 KB increments of data. So, a request with 513 KB of data is metered as two requests.

components.text: Select the units, number, and frequency for HTTP API requests based on expected volume. The calculations below exclude free tier discounts.

components.label: HTTP API requests units
components.description: The unit will apply to the number of requests you will specify below.

components.label: Requests
components.placeholder: Enter number of HTTP API requests in units selected

components.label: Average size of each request
components.description: Requests are defined as having 512 KB of data; a request with 513 KB of data is metered as two requests.

helpText.aws: With REST APIs, you only pay when your APIs are in use. There are no minimum fees or upfront commitments. You pay for the API calls you receive and the amount of data transferred out. There are no data transfer out charges for Private APIs. However, AWS PrivateLink charges apply when using Private APIs in API Gateway. API Gateway also provides optional data caching charged at an hourly rate that varies based on the cache size you select.
helpText.aws-iso: With REST APIs, you only pay when your APIs are in use. There are no minimum fees or upfront commitments. You pay for the API calls you receive and the amount of data transferred out. There are no data transfer out charges for Private APIs. However, AWS PrivateLink charges apply when using Private APIs in API Gateway. API Gateway also provides optional data caching charged at an hourly rate that varies based on the cache size you select.
helpText.aws-iso-b: With REST APIs, you only pay when your APIs are in use. There are no minimum fees or upfront commitments. You pay for the API calls you receive and the amount of data transferred out. There are no data transfer out charges for Private APIs. However, AWS PrivateLink charges apply when using Private APIs in API Gateway. API Gateway also provides optional data caching charged at an hourly rate that varies based on the cache size you select.
helpText.aws-cn: With REST APIs, you only pay when your APIs are in use. There are no minimum fees or upfront commitments. You pay for the API calls you receive and the amount of data transferred out. There are no data transfer out charges for Private APIs. However, Amazon Web Service PrivateLink charges apply when using Private APIs in API Gateway. API Gateway also provides optional data caching charged at an hourly rate that varies based on the cache size you select.

cards.title: WebSocket APIs
cards.helpText: With WebSocket APIs, you only pay when your APIs are in use. There are no minimum fees or upfront commitments. You pay for messages sent and received and the total number of connection minutes. You may send and receive messages up to 128 kilobytes (KB) in size. Messages are metered in 32 KB increments. So, a 33 KB message is metered as two messages.

components.text: Select the units, number, and frequency for WebSocket messages (sent and received) based on expected volume. The calculations below exclude free tier discounts.

components.label: WebSocket message units
components.description: The unit will apply to the number of messages you will specify below.

components.label: Messages
components.placeholder: Enter number of WebSocket messages in units selected

components.label: Average message size
components.description: You may send and receive messages up to 128 kilobytes (KB) in size. Messages are metered in 32 KB increments. So, a 33 KB message is metered as two messages.
components.help: Messages are metered in 32 KB increments. So, a 33 KB message is metered as two messages.

components.label: Average connection duration
components.description: Enter the average duration for each new connection (if the duration is less than 1 second then enter 1 second).

components.label: Average connection rate
components.placeholder: Enter the average number of connections
components.description: Average number of connections per second, minute, hour, or day.

components.label: Tiered price total for WebSocket messages
